questions.text: Which element form an ion with charge +3:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It belongs to group 2A so it has +2 charge
It belongs to group 3A so it has +3 charge
It belongs to group 4A so it has +4 charge
It belongs to group 4A so it has +4 charge

questions.explanation: The Group IIIA of the Periodic Table comprises the elements boron, aluminium, gallium, indium and thallium. As Al belongs to 3A group, it has 3 electron in valence shell so its ion has +3 charge.

questions.text: Which of the following element is not present abundantly in earth’s crust:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Sodium is not present abundantly in the earth's crustThe Earth's crust is a thin outer layer that makes up less than 1% of the planet's total volume. It is the top half of the lithosphere, which is made up of the crust and the upper part of the mantle and is a division of the layers of the Earth.95% of the rocks that make up the Earth's crust are igneous and metamorphic, while 4% are shale, 0.75% are sandstone, and 0.25% are limestone. 88.1% of the mass of the Earth's crust is made up of the elements oxygen, silicon, aluminum, and iron, and the remaining 11.9 percent is made up of another 90 elements.47% of the Earth's crust is oxygen, 28% is silicon, and 8.1% is aluminum. Only 2.8% of the Earth's crust is sodium

questions.text: Which of the following radicals give blue colour (in cold and hot state) in oxidizing flame when subjected to Borax Bead test:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Copper in form of cupric oxide gives blue colour in oxidizing flame

questions.explanation: Chemistry of the Borax-bead Test: Borax, when fused, is decomposed into sodium metaborate and boric anhydride. The metallic oxide formed from the substance, under examination, combines with B2O3 giving the coloured metallic borates. With cupric oxide, the beads are coloured blue in the oxidizing flame because cupric borates are blue in colour.

questions.text: Sulphur is not present in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is present in onion
It is present in garlic
It is present inegg
Sulphur is not present in fat

questions.explanation: Sulphur also occurs in organic compounds present in animals and vegetables. Onions, garlic, mustard, hair, many oils, eggsand proteins consist of compounds containing sulphur in them . It also occurs as a constituent of coal and petroleum .

questions.text: Which is the formula of clay:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Clay formula: Al2O3 . 2SiO2 . 2H2OTable 3.3 From Punjab board textbook

questions.text: BF3 acts as Lewis acid because it behaves as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: BF3 act as lewis acid because it accept electron just as electrophile it is electron loving

questions.explanation: Electrophile means electron loving so it accept electron pair.BF3 act as lewis acid because it accept electron to complete the octet and behave as electrophile

questions.text: Which of the following gas will turns lime water milky:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It will form calcium cholride
It does not form milky solution
Carbon dioxide reacts not carbonmonooxide to form milky solution
When lime water react with carbondioxide it turns it into milky solution

questions.explanation: Reaction with limewaterCarbon dioxide reacts with limewater (a solution of calcium hydroxide, Ca(OH)2), to form a whiteprecipitate (appears milky) of calcium carbonate,CaCO3.

questions.text: Elements which exhibits maximum catenation property:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon exhibit catenation property
It is not an element
It does not exhibit self linking properties
It is not an element

questions.explanation: Catenation or self linkage. Carbon has a tendency to form long chains of identical atoms. The type of linkage of identical atoms with each other is called catenation or self-linkage.The property of catenation decreases on moving down the group from carbon to lead.The maximum tendency of catenation associated with carbon forms the basis of the carbon compounds which constitute organicchemistry.

questions.text: Chrome yellow is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: PbCrO4 is used as a pigment under name of chrome yellow

questions.explanation: Lead Chromate (PbCrO4) It is used as a pigment under the name of chrome yellow. Orange or red basic lead chromates are formed when lead chromate is boiled with dilute alkali hydroxide and are used as pigments. The stable yellow modification of lead chromate is monoclinic. Mixture of lead chromate with lead sulphate or barium sulphate are also used as yellow pigments.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the formula of litharge:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lead monoxide PbO is also known as litharge

questions.explanation: Lead Monoxide (Litharge, Massicot) PbOLitharge varies in colour from pale yellow to reddish yellow, possibly owing to the existence of two forms, a rhombic (yellow) and a tetragonal (red). It is slightly soluble in water. It is usually used in preparing flint glass and paints. If litharge is boiled with water and olive oil, lead oleate which is a sticky adhesive mass is formed and glycerin passes into solution. Litharge is used in preparing oils and varnishes and in the manufacturing of flint glass.

questions.text: A gas which burn with blue flame is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon monoxide burns with blue flame

questions.explanation: In the presence of oxygen, including atmospheric concentrations, carbon monoxide burns with a blue flame, producing carbon dioxide.

questions.text: Which compound of silicon used as filler in soap to make it heavy:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium silicates is used as a filler for soap in soap industry

questions.explanation: Uses of Sodium Silicate:1. It is used as a filler for soap in soap industry.2. It is used in textile as a fire proof.3. It is used as furniture polish.4. It is also used in calico printing.

questions.text: Pb3O4 has chemical name of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sandhur is the red compound of lead in form Triplumbic tetraoxide ( Pb304)

questions.explanation: When lead is heated in air at about 340°C, it absorbs oxygen and forms a bright scarlet crystalline powder of read lead or minium. This red colour compound is used as sandhur whose chemical formula is Pb3O4 Triplumbic tetraoxide

questions.text: Chief ore of Al is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is not a chief ore
Bauxite is the chief ore of aluminium
It is not a chief ore
It is not a chief ore

questions.explanation: Bauxite (Al2O3. 2H2O) is the chief ore of aluminium from which aluminium is extracted by bayers process.

questions.text: Tincal is a mineral of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is not a mineral of Al
Tincal is a mineral of boron
It is not a mineral of Si
It is not a mineral of carbon

questions.explanation: Borax is the sodium salt of tetraboric acid. It is the most important of all borates .Borax occurs as a natural deposit called tincal in the dried up lakes of Tibet and California.Borax may also be obtained from tincal (Na2B4O7.10H2O) by treating tincalwith water and subsequently evaporating the clear solution, when crystalsof borax separate out.

questions.text: What is the nature of solution of Borax:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is not neutral as it ionizes to little extent
As it is ionized to a little extent so it is a weak acid and more alkaline
It is weak acidic

questions.explanation: Borax when dissolved in water form NaOH and H3BO3. a strong alkali (NaOH) is formed which is highly ionized. On the other hand,boric acid (H3BO3) is ionized to a little extent, because it is a weak acid. Hence,solution of borax as a whole is alkaliine in nature.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct number of lone-pair with oxygen in CO:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carbon and oxygen in CO form two covalent bond and one dativebond and then their is one lone pair left on oxygenExplained: oxygen has two lone pairs but no vacant orbitals. The oxygen thus transfers its one lone pair on to the vacant orbital of carbon forming a dative bond. Thus, Carbon and Oxygen have one lone pair each even after the formation

questions.text: Which metal is used in the thermit process because of its activity:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Iron is less reactive or has less oxidizing power than Al
the reaction does not take place if we use copper
Al has a high affinity for oxygen It readily removes oxygen from Oxides of less reactive metals so it is used in thermite process
In has low boiling point which is why it can potentially be superheated if it undergoes thermite reaction

questions.explanation: Al has a high affinity for oxygen It readily removes oxygen from Oxides of less reactive metals so it is used in thermite processWhen a piece of aluminium sheet is exposed to moist air it acquires a thin,continuous coating of aluminium oxide, which prevents further attack on the metal by atmospheric oxygen and water under normal conditions.Because of this aluminium sheets are said to be corrosion-free. However, if the aluminium powder is heated to 800°C and above, the metal will reactwith air to form aluminium oxide, Al2O3. The reaction is accompanied by the evolution of heat and intense white light.This property of aluminium is made use of in flash light photography

questions.text: Aluminium oxide is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Because it can act as acidic and basic

questions.explanation: Oxides of relatively less electropositive elements, such as BeO, Al2O3 Bi2O3 and ZnO are amphoteric and behave as acids towards strong bases and as bases towards strong acids.

questions.text: Chemical composition of colemanite is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

It is not colemanite
It is not colemanite

questions.explanation: Colemanite chemical formula is Ca2B6O11 . 5H2O

questions.text: Which electronic configuration corresponds to an element of group-IIIA of the periodic table:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: ns² np¹ belongs to group 3A
In valence shell there are two electron so it is group 2A
It has 8 electron in valence shell so it is group 8A
It has 5 electron in valence shell so it is 5A group

questions.explanation: As group 3A elements has 3 electrons in valence shell so its outer shell electronic configuration will be ns²np¹

questions.text: Which element among the following belongs to group IVA of the periodic table:

It belongs to group 7A
It belongs to group 4A
It belongs to group 6A

questions.explanation: Carbon, silicon, germanium, Lead belongs to group 4A as it has 4 electron in valance shell

questions.text: Boric acid cannot be used:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Uses of Boric Acid1. Boric acid is used in medicines as an antiseptic, e.g. dusting powder, boricointment and boric solution is used as an eye-wash.2. It is used in pottery as a glaze because borate glazes are more fusible thansilicate glazes and possess a higher coefficient of expansion.3. It is also used in candle industry for stiffening of wicks.

questions.text: Which of the following elements is not present abundantly in earth’s crust:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is not abundant in earth crust
It is not abundant in earth crust
Sodium belongs to group 1A so it is alkali metals
It is abundant in air

questions.explanation: The s-block elements are the metals in Group IA and Group IIA of the periodictable.They are called the s-block elements because sorbitals are being filled, in their outer most shells.The elements of group IA except hydrogen arecalled “Alkali metals” while those of IIA are named “Alkaline-earth metals”.The name alkali came from Arabic, which means ‘The Ashes’. The Arabs used this term for these metals because they found that the ashes of plants were composed chiefly of sodium and potassium. Alkali metals include the elements, lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium and francium. These are very reactive metals, produce strong alkaline solutions with water.The alkaline-earth metals are beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium and radium.They are called alkaline-earth because they produce alkalies in water and are widely distributed in earth’s crust.

options.explanation: Bauxite is the chief ore of aluminium

questions.explanation: Bauxite (Al2O3. 2H2O) is the chief ore of aluminium from which aluminium is extracted by bayers process

However,if the aluminium powder is heated to 800°C and above, the metal will react with air to form aluminium oxide, Al2O3. Thereaction is accompanied by the evolution of heat and intense white light.This property of aluminium is made use of in flash light photography,

questions.text: Aluminum oxide is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Al is not only acidic
Al is not only basic
Al reacts with both acidic and basic

questions.explanation: Aluminium is amphoteric. It dissolves in both acids and bases with the liberation of hydrogen gas.Aluminium reacts slowly with dilute acid and more rapidly with concentrated hydrochloric acid to displace hydrogen.Aluminium dissolves in both sodium and potassium hydroxides to form a soluble aluminate, with the evolution of hydrogen.

questions.text: Stable shape in which PbCrO4 exist:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is unstable
The stable yellow modification of lead chromate is monoclinic.
It is unstable

questions.explanation: Lead Chromate (PbCrO4) It is used as a pigment under the name of chrome yellow. Orange orred basic lead chromates are formed when lead chromate is boiled with dilute alkali hydroxide and are used as pigments. The stable yellow modification of lead chromate is monoclinic. Mixture of lead chromate with lead sulphate or barium sulphate are also used as yellow pigments.
